summary: Clear soundboard capture of a fiery late‑'72 Texas show, highlighted by a scorching Candyman, an incandescent 29‑minute The Other One and a sweet, soaring Stella vocal.
review: The recording is a warm, roomy soundboard that’s mostly clear – early mix quirks and distant drums are noted, but the overall balance lets the band’s peak‑era energy shine through. Fans rave about the first‑set fire (Candyman, El Paso, Bird Song) and the second‑set monsters (Playing in the Band, He's Gone, Truckin', a 29‑minute Other One, Stella and an instrument‑less I Know You Rider). Individual performances from Jerry, Phil, Bob and Keith receive particular praise.

ai_rating.rationale: The majority of reviews (13/16) are 4‑5 stars and extol the musical performance; technical comments note only minor mix issues, yielding a strong overall rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Warm, roomy feel with generally clear up‑front mix. Early part of the show suffers from mix problems and distant drums; Donna’s vocal on Playing in the Band is described as harsh. Track‑number metadata missing for some releases.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set fires with crowd‑pleasers (Candyman, El Paso, Bird Song) and a strong Playing in the Band; second set builds into extended jams – especially a 29‑minute Other One that many cite as the highlight – before a beautiful segue into Stella and an instrumentless I Know You Rider finale.

band_member_comments.Jerry: White‑hot, cool‑as‑ice licks praised on Playing in the Band; his vocal on Stella called "sweet" and "goosebump‑inducing".
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass meditation highlighted during Playing in the Band; thumb‑popping groove on The Other One; “bangs” on Black Throated Wind noted.
band_member_comments.Bob: Drums described as distant early on but powerful later, especially driving the energy on The Other One and Truckin'.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith’s piano arpeggios before I Know You Rider called "absurd" and his flanged‑wah sound on Candyman praised; overall rhythm work noted as a perfect counterpoint to Garcia.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill (Bob) receives praise for hot, propulsive playing throughout the set.
